volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: The Casket Comedy (Section 2.1.505b - Section 2.3.595b)

sections.label: Section 2.1.505b
sections.original: Inter novam rem verbum usurpabo vetus :
sections.translation: Under new circumstances I ’ ll use an old proverb : What I have given , I wish I had not given ; what ’ s left , that I shall not give .

sections.label: Section 2.1.507
sections.translation: Won ’ t you restore her again to me ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.507b
sections.translation: Answer yourself for me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.508
sections.translation: You won ’ t restore her then ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.508b
sections.original: Scis iam dudum omnem meam sententiam .
sections.translation: You know the whole of my resolution already .

sections.label: Section 2.1.509
sections.original: Satin istuc tibi in corde certumst ?
sections.translation: Is that quite resolved upon by you in your heart ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.509b
sections.translation: Why , in fact , I ’ m thinking about something else ;

sections.label: Section 2.1.510
sections.original: Non edepol ego istaec tua dicta nunc in auris recipio .
sections.translation: i ’ faith , I don ’ t at present catch these words of yours with my ears .

sections.label: Section 2.1.511
sections.original: Non ? hem , quid agis igitur ? animum advorte iam , ut quid agas scias .
sections.translation: Not hear ? Why , what are you doing ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.512
sections.original: At ita me di deaeque , superi atque inferi et medioxumi ,
sections.translation: Then , so may the Gods and Goddesses of above and below , and of middle rank [*] , and so may Juno the queen and the daughter [*] of supreme Jove , and so may Saturn his uncle —

sections.label: Section 2.1.514b
sections.translation: I ’ troth , his father —

sections.label: Section 2.1.515
sections.original: Itaque me Ops opulenta illius avia —
sections.translation: And so may Ops the opulent , his grandam —

sections.label: Section 2.1.515b
sections.translation: Indeed , his mother , rather .

sections.label: Section 2.1.516
sections.original: Iuno filia et Saturnus patruos et summus Iuppiter —
sections.translation: Juno his daughter , and Saturn his uncle , supreme Jove — You are maddening me ; it ’ s through you I make these mistakes .

sections.label: Section 2.1.517b
sections.translation: Go on saying so .

sections.label: Section 2.1.518
sections.original: Anne etiam , ut quid consultura sis sciam , pérgis eloqui ?
sections.translation: Is it that I ’ m to know [*] what conclusion you are going to come to ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.519
sections.translation: Go on talking ; I shall not send her back , that ’ s resolved upon .

sections.label: Section 2.1.519b
sections.translation: Why then , so may Jupiter ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.520
sections.original: itaque me Iuno itaque Ianus ita — quid dicam nescio .
sections.translation: and so may Juno and Saturn , to me , so may — I don ’ t know what to say — Now I know — Yes , madam , listen , that you may know my mind ; may all the Deities , great and small , and those honored with the platter [*] ⋯ cause me not surviving to give a kiss this day to Silenium , if I don ’ t this very day murder you and your daughter and myself ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.525
sections.original: poste autem cum primo luci cras nisi ambo occidero ,
sections.translation: and after that , with the break of day , if I don ’ t to-morrow kill you both , and indeed , by all the powers , if at the third onset I don ’ t demolish you all , if you don ’ t send her back to me . I ’ ve said what I intended . Farewell .

sections.label: Section 2.1.528
sections.original: Abiit intro iratus . quid ego nunc agam ? si redierit
sections.translation: He ’ s gone in-doors in a rage . What shall I do now ? If she comes back to him , matters will be just in the same position . When satiety begins to take possession ;

sections.label: Section 2.1.530
sections.original: illam extrudet , tum hanc uxorem Lemniam ducet domum .
sections.translation: he ’ ll be turning her out of doors , when he shall be bringing home this Lemnian wife . But still I ’ ll go and follow him ; there ’ s necessity for caution , lest he , in love , should be doing some mischief . In fine , since with strict justice a poor person ’ s not allowed to contend with a rich one , I ’ ll lose my labour rather than lose my daughter . But who ’ s this that straight along the street is directing his course this way ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.535
sections.original: et illud paveo et hoc formido , ita tota sum misera in metu .
sections.translation: Both the other matter do I fear , and this do I dread ; so utterly in trepidation am wretched I .

sections.label: Section 2.2.536
sections.original: Anum sectatus sum clamore per vias ,
sections.translation: I ’ ve followed the old woman with my clamour through the streets ; I ’ ve kept her most dreadfully plagued . In what a multitude of ways has she , this day , kept guard upon herself , and been able to remember nothing . How many alluring things , what advantages I ’ ve promised her .

sections.label: Section 2.2.540
sections.translation: How many inventions I ’ ve applied to her , how many stratagems in questioning her . With difficulty have I extorted it from her that she should tell me , because I promised to give her a cask of wine .

sections.label: Section 2.3.543
sections.original: Audire vocem visa sum ante aedis modo
sections.translation: I seemed just now to be hearing the voice of my servant Lampadiscus before the house .

sections.label: Section 2.3.544b
sections.translation: You are not deaf , mistress , you heard aright .

sections.label: Section 2.3.545b
sections.translation: What are you doing here ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.545c
sections.translation: A thing for you to rejoice at .

sections.label: Section 2.3.546
sections.translation: What ’ s that ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.546b
sections.original: Hinc ex hisce aedibus paulo prius
sections.translation: A little while ago , I saw a woman coming out of that house there .

sections.label: Section 2.3.547b
sections.translation: Her that took up my daughter ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.548b
sections.translation: You have the matter rgiht .

sections.label: Section 2.3.548c
sections.translation: What after that ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.549
sections.original: Dico éi , quo pacto eam ab hippodromo viderim
sections.translation: I told her in what way I had seen her take up the daughter of my mistress from the Hippodrome .

sections.label: Section 2.3.551
sections.translation: Then she was in a fright .

sections.label: Section 2.3.551b
sections.translation: ( apart .) Now my body ’ s in a shudder , my heart is throbbing ; for I recollect that from the Hippodrome the little female infant was brought to me , and that I brought it up as my own .

sections.label: Section 2.3.554
sections.original: Age perge , quaeso . ánimus audire expetit
sections.translation: Come , prithee , do go on ; my soul ’ s longing to hear

sections.label: Section 2.3.555
sections.translation: how the matter proceeded .

sections.label: Section 2.3.555b
sections.translation: ( apart .) I only wish you couldn ’ t hear .

sections.label: Section 2.3.556
sections.original: Pergo illa m onerare dictis : “ illaec ted anus
sections.translation: I proceed ⋯ saying [*] , This old woman calls you her daughter wrongfully . ⋯ For this woman here is your foster-mother , so don ’ t think she is your mother . I ’ m to take you back and invite you to opulence , where you may be settled in a noble family ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.560
sections.original: ubi tu locere in luculentam familiam ,
sections.translation: where your father may present you with twenty great talents for a portion . For this is not a place where after the Etrurian mode [*] you are disgracefully to earn a dowry for yourself by prostitution of your person .

sections.label: Section 2.3.564
sections.original: An , amabo , meretrix illa est quae illam sustulit ?
sections.translation: Is she , pray , a Courtesan , who took it up ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.565
sections.original: Immo meretrix fuit ; sed ut sit dé ea re , eloquar .
sections.translation: Yes , she was a Courtesan . But how it happened , I ’ ll tell you about that matter . I was now winning her over to me by my persuasion . The old woman embraced her knees , weeping and entreating that she would not forsake her ; saying that she was her own daughter ; and she took a solemn oath to me that she herself had borne her .

sections.label: Section 2.3.570
sections.original: istanc quam quaeris inquit “ ego amicae meae
sections.translation: Her , said she , whom you are in search of , I gave to a friend of mine to bring her up as her own daughter ; and she is alive , said she . Where is she ? immediately said I .

sections.label: Section 2.3.573
sections.translation: Preserve me , ye Gods , I do entreat you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.573b
sections.translation: But me you are undoing !

sections.label: Section 2.3.574
sections.translation: You ought to have enquired to whom she gave it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.575
sections.translation: I did enquire , and she said to the Courtesan Melaenis .

sections.label: Section 2.3.576
sections.translation: He has mentioned my name ? I ’ m utterly undone !

sections.label: Section 2.3.577
sections.original: Vbi elocuta est , ego continuo anum interrogo ;
sections.translation: When she mentioned her , I straightway asked , Where does she live ? said I ; take and show me . She has been carried off hence , says she , to live abroad .

sections.label: Section 2.3.579b
sections.translation: He ’ s sprinkling [*] a little cold water now .

sections.label: Section 2.3.580b
sections.original: Quo avecta est , eo sequemur . sicine
sections.translation: Wherever she has been carried off , thither we will follow . Do you trifle in this fashion ? You are undone , if , i ’ faith , you don ’ t disclose this . I insisted to such a degree , that the old woman swore that she would soon inform me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.584b
sections.translation: But you oughtn ’ t to have let her go .

sections.label: Section 2.3.585
sections.original: Servatur . sed illaec se quandam aibat mulierem
sections.translation: She ’ s all safe ; but she said that she wished first to meet a certain woman , a friend of hers , with whom this was a matter of interest in common , and I ’ m sure she ’ ll come .

sections.label: Section 2.3.588b
sections.translation: She ’ ll be discovering me , and adding her own distress to mine .

sections.label: Section 2.3.591
sections.translation: Make me acquainted what you now wish me to do .

sections.label: Section 2.3.591b
sections.original: Intro abi atque animo bono es .
sections.translation: Go in-doors , and be of good heart . If your husband shall come , bid him wait at home , lest he should be required by me , if I want him for anything . I ’ m going to run back to the old woman .

sections.label: Section 2.3.594b
sections.translation: Lampadio , prithee , do take care .

sections.label: Section 2.3.595b
sections.translation: I ’ ll have this matter well managed .
